description: Under Illinois law, the Treasurer’s Office is required to hold an Annual Tax Sale. The sale is held once a year, sometime after the Second Installment. If property taxes for the immediately preceding tax year are delinquent on a parcel, they are offered for sale to tax purchasers at the Annual Tax Sale. columns.name: Age
columns.description: Age of the property. If missing, this defaults to 10. This field is a combination of original age and effective age where original age refers to the oldest component of the building and effective age is a relative judgement due to renovations or other improvements. For instance, if a property is completely demolished and built up again, the age resets to 1. But if portions of the original structure are kept, it may be more complicated to determine the age.

columns.name: Total Building Square Feet
columns.description: This field applies to condominium properties (299) only. It is the sum of interior square feet of all units in the building.

columns.name: Modeling Group
columns.description: Modeling group, as defined by the property class. Properties with class 200, 201, 241, 299 is defined as "NCHARS", short for "no characteristics", which are condos and vacant land classes.  Properties with class 202, 203, 204, 205, 206, 207, 208, 209, 210, 235, 278, and 295 are "SF", short for "single-family."  Properties with class 211 and 212 are "MF", short for ""multi-family."

description: Neighborhood polygons used by the Cook County Assessor's Office for valuation and reporting. These neighborhoods are specific to the Assessor. They are intended to represent homogenous housing submarkets, NOT Chicago community areas or municipalities. These neighborhoods were reconstructed from individual parcels using spatial buffering and simplification. The full transformation script can be found on the Assessor's GitHub. Read about the Assessor's 2025 Open Data Refresh.

columns.name: Age Adjusted Mortality Rate
columns.description: Age-adjustment is a statistical process applied mortality rates which allows communities with different age structures to be compared. Adjustment is accomplished by first multiplying the age-specific rates of death by age-specific weights based off of the 2010 standard population. The weighted rates are then summed across the age groups.

columns.name: Full Baths
columns.description: Number of full bathrooms, defined as having a bath or shower. If this value is missing, the default value is set to 1.

columns.name: Condo Model: Modeled Unit Strata (of 300)
columns.description: In addition to dividing up all condo sale prices into 10 bins, or strata, we also divided all condo sale prices into 300 strata.

columns.name: Condo Model: Building PIN
columns.description: This is the PIN of the condo's building.

columns.name: Tract Median Income
columns.description: Median income of the census tract containing the property centroid.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Longitude coordinate of the property's location, as defined by the centroid of the parcel shape in GIS.

columns.name: High School District
columns.description: High school district boundaries for Cook County and CPS.

columns.name: Flood Risk Factor
columns.description: The property's First Street Flood Factor, a numeric integer from 1-10 (where 1 = minimal and 10 = extreme) based on flooding risk to the building footprint. Flood risk is defined as a combination of cumulative risk over 30 years and flood depth. Flood depth is calculated at the lowest elevation of the building footprint (largest if more than 1 exists, or property centroid where footprint does not exist. Data provided by First Street and academics at UPenn.

columns.name: Tract GEOID
columns.description: Full 11-digit GEOID for matched census tract.

columns.name: Property Zip Code
columns.description: Property location: zip code.

columns.name: Type of Residence
columns.description: Residences with 1.5 - 1.9 stories are one story and have partial livable attics and are classified based on the square footage of the attic compared to the first floor of the house. So, 1.5 story houses have an attic that is 50% of the area of the first floor, 1.6 story houses are 60%, 1.7 are 70%, etc. However, what is recorded on the field card differs from what is in the database. All 1.5 - 1.9 story houses are coded as 5

columns.name: Building Square Feet
columns.description: As measured from the exterior of the building.

columns.name: Bedrooms
columns.description: Number of bedrooms in the property, defined based on building square footage and the judgement of the person in the field.

columns.name: Land Square Feet
columns.description: Square feet of the land (not just the building) of the property. Note that land is divided into 'plots' and 'parcels' - this field applies to parcels, identified by PIN.

columns.name: Sale Document Number
columns.description: Document number of a property sale, as recorded by the Clerk's office.

columns.name: Sale Price
columns.description: Market price of sale.

columns.name: Sale Date
columns.description: Date of sale.

columns.name: Multi Code
columns.description: Field that serves as a key when multiple buildings or improvements exist on the same PIN. Numeric values do not necessary correspond to number of buildings. Can also represent improvements added through Home Improvement Exemptions (288s).

columns.name: Proportion Assessed
columns.description: Proportion (0.5 = 50%) assessed for prorated properties. Percent of ownership of a condo building for a unit as defined by the condominium declaration. If missing, default is 1/n_units. Note: Percent Assessed corresponds to this PIN's percentage of the total building for purposes of market value calculation. This is distinct from the Level of Assessment. In Cook County, residential properties like those in this dataset all have a Level of Assessment of 10%, such that the Assessed Value equals 10% of Market Value.

columns.name: Township Neighborhood Only
columns.description: The neighborhood printed on assessment notices.

columns.name: Neighborhood Code
columns.description: Assessor neighborhood. First 2 digits are township code, last 3 digits are neighborhood code. This field can be used for mapping to the Neighborhood Boundaries shape file on Open Data.

columns.name: Property City
columns.description: Property location: city.

columns.name: Property Index Number
columns.description: All PINs are 14 digits: 2 digits for area + 2 digits for sub area + 2 digits for block + 2 digits for parcel + 4 digits for the multicode

columns.name: Modeled Sale Quarter of Year
columns.description: Character encoding of quarter of year (Q1 - Q4).

columns.name: Modeled Sale Week of Year
columns.description: Numeric encoding of week of year (1 - 52).

columns.name: Garage 1 Area
columns.description: Is Garage 1 physically including within the building area? If yes, the garage area is subtracted from the building square feet calculation by the field agent.

columns.name: Property Address
columns.description: Property street address, not the address of the taxpayer.

columns.name: Modeled Sale During Holidays
columns.description: Indicator for whether sale occurred during holiday season (November - January).

columns.name: Modeled Sale Month of Year
columns.description: Character encoding of month of year (Jan - Dec).

columns.name: Condo Model: Modeled Unit Strata (of 10)
columns.description: Condo units are assigned to a strata (1 through 10) that indicates the range of the 5-year average sale price of units in this strata. All units in a condo building will have the same strata.

columns.name: Tax Rate
columns.description: Tax rate paid by the property owner. Taken from Cook County Treasurer's Office.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude coordinate of the property's location, as defined by the centroid of the parcel shape in GIS.

columns.name: Elementary/Middle School District
columns.description: Elementary and middle school district boundaries for Cook County and CPS.

columns.name: Road Proximity < 100 Feet
columns.description: Indicates whether the property is within 100 feet of a major road.

columns.name: Flood Risk Direction
columns.description: The property's flood risk direction represented in a numeric value based on the change in risk for the location from 2020 to 2050 for the climate model realization of the RCP 4.5 mid emissions scenario. -1 = descreasing, 0 = stationary, 1 = increasing. Data provided by First Street and academics at UPenn.

columns.name: FEMA Floodplain
columns.description: Indicator for whether property lies within a FEMA-defined floodplain.

columns.name: Municipality FIPS Code
columns.description: FIPS code of the municipality containing the property.

columns.name: Repair Condition
columns.description: This field is subjective and contains little variation. As such, it is not used for modeling.

columns.name: Half Baths
columns.description: Number of half baths, defined as bathrooms without a shower or bathtub.

columns.name: Large Home Indicator
columns.description: If true, property class is either 208 or 209.

columns.name: Modeling Group
columns.description: Modeling group, as defined by the property class. Properties with class 200, 201, 241, 299 is defined as "NCHARS", short for "no characteristics", which are condos and vacant land classes. Properties with class 202, 203, 204, 205, 206, 207, 208, 209, 210, 235, 278, and 295 are "SF", short for "single-family." Properties with class 211 and 212 are "MF", short for "multi-family." Properties with class 218 and 219 are "BB", short for "bed and breakfast."

columns.name: Property Apartment Num.

columns.name: Property Class
columns.description: Property's classification code, according to Cook County's classification system.

columns.name: Modeled Sale Month
columns.description: Sale month calculated as the number of months since January 1997.

columns.description: Numeric encoding of day of year (1 - 365).

columns.name: Modeled Sale Year

columns.name: Road Proximity 101 - 300 Feet

columns.name: Cathedral Ceiling
columns.description: This field name comes from a variable that is no longer used, but the field name was not changed to reflect Cathedral Ceiling

columns.name: Fireplaces
columns.description: Number of fireplaces, counted as the number of flues one can see from the outside of the building.

columns.name: Number of Active 288s (HIEs)
columns.description: Number of 288s (Home Improvement Exemptions) active for this property in the given year.

columns.name: Multi Code Indicator
columns.description: Indicates a property with multiple improvements on one PIN, e.g. a main house and a coach house. NOT to be confused with a property which was part of a multi-pin sale.

columns.name: Assessment Triad
columns.description: Cook County is divided into three assessment triads: North Suburbs, South/West Suburbs, and the City (of Chicago). Each is reassessed every three years.

columns.name: Modeled Sale During School Year
columns.description: Indicator for whether sale occurred during usual school year (September - May).

columns.name: Modeled Sale Quarter
columns.description: Sale quarter calculated as the number of quarters since January 1997.

columns.name: Number of Ended 288s (HIEs)
columns.description: Number of 288s (Home Improvement Exemptions) that ended in the given year. This is effectively an indicator for properties that have updated characteristics due to a 288 expiring.

description: Annual Tax Sale: A yearly auction of delinquent taxes where a tax buyer (purchaser) pays the delinquent taxes due on a parcel. This results in a lien on the property. The property owner must repay the tax buyer with interest for the amount of the sale – this is known as “redemption.” If redemption does not occur within a set time period, the tax buyer can obtain legal title to the property – this is referred to as a “tax deed.”

columns.name: OFFENSE TYPE
columns.description: Type of offense for the “Topline” charge. Many cases involve multiple charges stemming from the same incident. On a charging document, these various charges are called “counts.” This data report is based on Count 1 for each case, which is generally the most serious charge in a given case, and is commonly referred to as the “topline charge.”

columns.name: AVERAGE CASE LENGTH
columns.description: Average length in days of the case from initiation to sentencing

columns.name: YEAR
columns.description: The year in which time was spent on the case.

columns.name: SUM OF LENGTH
columns.description: The sum length in days of cases from initiation to sentencing

description: Sum of Length is an internal calculation used to produce the Average Case Length in a specific offense category. Neither should be read to be associated with any individual case. Rather, the Sum of Length is the number of days in total spent on all the cases in which defendants were charged in a specific charge category combined. The Average Case Length in a particular charge category is then derived by dividing the Sum of Length by the number of defendants.

name: ccgisdata - TIF_2015
description: TIF District boundaries. TIF districts receive money from property taxes by utilizing increases in the value of properties located in the TIF. There is no tax rate for TIF districts. Instead, TIFs receive money based on tax rates generated by other districts’ tax levies. Money is allocated to the TIF based on the composite tax rate for properties in the TIF and the incremental value of properties in that TIF (when compared to values when the TIF was established.)

description: This dataset indicates the services that the County expects to procure in 2026, allowing vendors to prepare for upcoming solicitations.
